danger_signals.visual_form: Puyos stacking upward in central column reaching play area ceiling
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent game over condition; player losing state

danger_signals.visual_form: Ojama (obstacle) pieces descending from top after opponent chain
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent has successfully chained; incoming threat to board space

danger_signals.visual_form: Visually distinct garbage blocks harder to erase than standard puyos
Puyo pieces accumulating toward top of play field

safety_signals.visual_form: Special point-awarding puyos disappear with adjacent group matches
safety_signals.meaning: Positive scoring event; short-term chain power boost

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple puyos cleared simultaneously; cascading elimination visual
safety_signals.meaning: Player advantage; damage dealt to opponent via garbage send

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear play field with minimal puyos present
safety_signals.meaning: Safety; breathing room for new piece placement

safety_signals.visual_form: Garbage blocks negated/cancelled by player's own chain

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Battle Tower setting; climbing progression through tower floors

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dark Prince character representing opposition faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tower constructor; initiates battle tower structure as antagonistic setup

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Battle Tower structure visible; climbing progression through sequential floors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Arle advances up tower by defeating opponents on each floor; bonus points accumulate as 'Experience'

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dual play fields side-by-side; garbage blocks transfer between players
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Garbage Puyos sent from opponent to player; offsetting mechanic allows counter-sends

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four simultaneous play fields visible; multitap required (Super Famicom version) or CPU fill-in (Remix)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All players competing simultaneously; cascading garbage sends to multiple opponents

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hard Puyos (Steelies) introduced to play field; visual distinction from standard garbage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Board becomes harder to clear; standard removal tactics less effective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Extended chain cascade; Point Puyos cleared adding visual confirmation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High-value garbage dump to opponent; opponent board pressure increases

environmental_storytelling.example: Dark Prince constructs tower as romantic competition mechanism (steal Arle's heart AND Carbuncle); tower climbing represents escalating challenge and narrative progression
Each tower floor contains a unique opponent character Arle must defeat; visual/mechanical progression mirrors narrative escalation
Victory bonuses convert to 'Experience' points; quantified progression communicates player advancement through story
Ojama, Point Puyos, and Hard Puyos each have distinct visual appearance; enables quick danger assessment without text

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Descend from top at consistent rate; player rotates/positions before landing
Spawn at top of opponent's field; descend as obstruction after opponent damage dealt
Award bonus points when cleared with adjacent puyos; enhance short-term chain power
Resist erasure; require higher match counts or offsetting to remove; increase board constraint
Climbs tower floors sequentially; gains bonus points per victory; narrative motivation to reach Dark Prince
Constructs tower; serves as final boss; antagonistic motivation (romance + Carbuncle theft)

notes: Puyo Puyo 2 uses minimal narrative/world-state communication compared to story-driven games. Visual differentiation primarily focuses on game mechanic clarity (garbage types, chain states) rather than environmental narrative. The battle tower and character opponent sequencing provide lightweight narrative framing. The offsetting mechanic and garbage overflow system create dynamic visual board-state communication of tactical pressure/advantage shifts. Faction identity is minimal—primarily antagonist (Dark Prince) vs. protagonist (Arle). World state manifests almost entirely through rule-set enforcement (offsetting rules, chain multipliers, custom rule variations) visible in board mechanics rather than environmental changes.
